function_calling.details: All 4 Fireworks models support OpenAI-style function calling (tools + tool_choice)

tested_scenarios.single_tool_auto: ✅ All 4 models called get_weather correctly with tool_choice=auto
tested_scenarios.forced_tool_call: ✅ All models honor tool_choice=required (glm-5p2, kimi-k2p7-code, deepseek-v4-pro tested)
tested_scenarios.parallel_multi_tool: ✅ glm-5p2, kimi-k2p7-code, deepseek-v4-pro all made 3 parallel calls (get_weather + get_time + search_web)
tested_scenarios.streaming_with_tools: ✅ tool_calls appear in SSE stream chunks (7 occurrences confirmed in glm-5p2 stream)

structured_output_json_schema.details: response_format: {type: 'json_schema', json_schema: {name, schema, strict}} works on all models

results.qwen3p7-plus: ⚠️ Partial - returned some fields but missed 'name' and 'age' (only returned city+country)

json_object_mode.details: response_format: {type: 'json_object'} works on ALL 4 models - all returned valid JSON

streaming.details: SSE streaming with stream=true. Proper data: prefixed chunks, [DONE] terminator

sampling_params.max_tokens: ✅ Passthrough works (tested 10 and 50 - exact token count matches)

logprobs.details: logprobs=true + top_logprobs=3 returns token logprobs with top alternatives

seed_reproducibility.details: seed=42 produces identical output across runs (tested on glm-5p2)

stop_sequences.details: stop=['5'] correctly stops generation and returns finish_reason=stop

prompt_caching.details: usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens shows cached input tokens (10/13 tokens cached on test)
prompt_caching.pricing_impact: Cached input at $0.14/M vs $1.40/M = 90% discount on cached tokens

system_prompt.details: System role messages pass through and are respected by all models

features_not_tested_but_available.vision_image_input: NOT TESTED - Fireworks supports vision models (Llama 3.2 Vision, Qwen2-VL) but our 4 models are text-only
features_not_tested_but_available.embeddings: Fireworks supports embeddings endpoints but our gateway doesn't expose them
features_not_tested_but_available.lora_fine_tuning: Fireworks supports LoRA adapters on deployed models - not applicable to serverless models we use
features_not_tested_but_available.speculative_decoding: Fireworks uses speculative decoding internally for faster inference - transparent to API
features_not_tested_but_available.prefetch: Fireworks has a prefetch API for reducing TTFT - not exposed in our gateway
features_not_tested_but_available.fim_fill_in_middle: Available on code models (some support FIM) - kimi-k2p7-code might support it

recommendations.feature: Update MODELS capabilities to reflect actual tested features
recommendations.reason: Our MODELS dict already has capabilities.tools=True for all Fireworks models, which is correct. But we should verify the /v1/models endpoint accurately advertises these.
recommendations.action: Verify /v1/models endpoint shows tools=true for all Fireworks models

recommendations.feature: Expose prompt caching info to users
recommendations.reason: Prompt caching gives 90% discount on cached tokens. Users should see cached_tokens in usage to understand cost savings.
recommendations.action: Our gateway already passes through usage data including prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens - this works transparently

recommendations.feature: Add Fireworks vision models (Llama 3.2 11B/90B Vision, Qwen2-VL)
recommendations.reason: Would add image understanding capability to the gateway
recommendations.action: Add new model entries with capabilities.vision=true, use image_url in messages

recommendations.reason: Fireworks offers embedding models (e.g. nomic-embed-text-v1, bge-large-en) for RAG/search
recommendations.action: Add /v1/embeddings endpoint that proxies to Fireworks /v1/embeddings

recommendations.feature: Add more Fireworks models to the gateway
recommendations.reason: Fireworks hosts many serverless models beyond our 4: Llama 4, Qwen3, DeepSeek V3, etc.
recommendations.action: Research and add high-value serverless models
Research /v1/prefetch endpoint - may need custom client integration

recommendations.reason: kimi-k2p7-code might support FIM for better code completion

summary.overall: All Fireworks features we tested are working through our gateway. The proxy is a clean passthrough — it forwards the request body as-is to Fireworks, so any OpenAI-compatible parameter (tools, tool_choice, response_format, stream, seed, logprobs, n, stop, etc.) passes through automatically. No code changes needed for function calling — it already works.
